instructions.instruction: Pin all action references to a full 40-character SHA with a version comment
instructions.original_snippets: - uses: actions/checkout@08c6903cd8c0fde910a37f88322edcfb5dd907a8 # v5.0.0 ... NEVER use `@latest` or branch-based action references
instructions.relevant_when: Whenever any `uses:` step references a third-party or GitHub action

instructions.instruction: Set a top-level `permissions:` block defaulting to `contents: read` and override per-job only where write access is genuinely required
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER omit `permissions:` at the job or workflow level ... Set `permissions: contents: read` as the workflow default
instructions.relevant_when: Every workflow that is generated

instructions.instruction: Add a `concurrency:` block to cancel in-progress runs when a new commit is pushed
instructions.relevant_when: CI workflows triggered by push or pull_request

instructions.instruction: Never use `secrets: inherit` in reusable workflows; declare only the required secrets explicitly
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use `secrets: inherit` in reusable workflows without justification ... Declare only required secrets explicitly
instructions.relevant_when: When authoring or calling reusable workflows with `workflow_call`

instructions.instruction: Use typed inputs and explicit outputs in reusable `workflow_call` workflows
instructions.original_snippets:
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      environment:
        required: true
        type: string
    secrets:
      deploy-token:
        required: true
    outputs:
      result:
        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.result }}
instructions.relevant_when: When generating reusable workflows

instructions.instruction: Enable dependency caching for the language ecosystem using either setup-action built-in caching or `actions/cache@v4`
instructions.original_snippets: cache: 'npm' ... actions/cache v4.2.0+ is required (v4.3.0 latest). The cache service was rewritten
instructions.relevant_when: CI workflows that install language dependencies (Node, Python, Java, Ruby, .NET)

instructions.instruction: Add `timeout-minutes` at the job level (and optionally at the step level) to prevent hung jobs
instructions.original_snippets: timeout-minutes: 30  # Prevent hung jobs
instructions.relevant_when: Any workflow with a build, test, or deploy job

instructions.instruction: Use environment variables instead of direct `${{ }}` interpolation of untrusted or user-supplied inputs in `run:` steps to prevent script injection
instructions.original_snippets: BEST: Always use environment variables for untrusted input ... ❌ BAD: Direct interpolation of user input (vulnerable to injection)
instructions.relevant_when: Any workflow step that uses `run:` and references `github.event.*` or user-supplied inputs

instructions.instruction: Never use `pull_request_target` combined with `actions/checkout` checking out PR head code
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use `pull_request_target` with `actions/checkout` checking out PR code ... BAD: `on: pull_request_target` combined with `uses: actions/checkout@... with: ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}`
instructions.relevant_when: When generating workflows that run on PRs from forks, or that use `pull_request_target`

instructions.instruction: Do not set `fail-fast: false` by default in matrix builds; omit it to use the default `true`, or add an explicit comment if intentional
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER set `fail-fast: false` by default in matrix builds ... Omit `fail-fast` to use the default `true`, or add an explicit comment
instructions.relevant_when: When generating matrix strategies

instructions.instruction: Use descriptive semantic names for workflows, jobs, and steps; workflow files must use lowercase-hyphen naming
instructions.original_snippets: Descriptive names, lowercase-hyphen files ... name: CI Pipeline ... test-node: ... Install dependencies
instructions.relevant_when: Every workflow generated

instructions.instruction: Add cleanup steps with `if: always()` for resources that must be torn down regardless of success or failure
instructions.relevant_when: Workflows that start external services, containers, or test environments

instructions.instruction: For security scanning workflows, set `permissions: security-events: write` for CodeQL and `id-token: write` plus `attestations: write` for SBOM attestations
instructions.original_snippets:
permissions:
  contents: read
  security-events: write  # For CodeQL
  id-token: write         # For attestations
  attestations: write
instructions.relevant_when: When generating security scanning or SBOM attestation workflows

instructions.instruction: Use `workflow_run` instead of `pull_request_target` when post-CI processing (e.g., coverage upload, commenting) needs secrets and must run after an external PR's CI completes
instructions.original_snippets: Safer than `pull_request_target` for external PRs: Runs with workflow file from target branch ... Use `workflow_run` instead of `pull_request_target` when possible
instructions.relevant_when: When generating workflows that need to react to fork PR CI results while retaining access to secrets

instructions.instruction: Use `actions/upload-artifact@v4` / `actions/download-artifact@v4` (not older versions) and name artifacts with `${{ github.sha }}` for uniqueness
instructions.original_snippets:
- uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: build-${{ github.sha }}
    path: dist/
instructions.relevant_when: Multi-job workflows that share build outputs between jobs

instructions.instruction: Use ChatOps `issue_comment` trigger with author-association permission checks before executing any sensitive operations
instructions.original_snippets: contains(fromJSON('["OWNER", "MEMBER", "COLLABORATOR"]'), github.event.comment.author_association) ... Never: Execute arbitrary code from comments ... Never: Trust external PR authors for sensitive operations
instructions.relevant_when: When generating ChatOps or comment-triggered workflows

instructions.instruction: Use `repository_dispatch` trigger for external system integrations; always validate `client_payload` fields and use allowlists for critical fields
instructions.original_snippets: Always validate `client_payload` fields ... Sanitize user input to prevent injection ... Use allowlists for critical fields
instructions.relevant_when: When generating workflows triggered from external systems or APIs

instructions.instruction: Prefer `actions/setup-*` built-in caching (`cache: 'npm'`) over manual `actions/cache` where available
instructions.original_snippets:
NPM/Node.js with Built-in Caching:
- uses: actions/setup-node@v6
  with:
    node-version: '24'
    cache: 'npm'
instructions.relevant_when: Workflows that set up Node, Java, .NET, or other runtimes that have setup actions with built-in caching

instructions.instruction: Use `mask` (`echo "::add-mask::$SECRET"`) for any secret value used inline in a `run:` step
instructions.original_snippets:
echo "::add-mask::$API_KEY"
./deploy.sh
instructions.relevant_when: Workflows that access secrets in shell `run:` steps

instructions.instruction: For dependency review, use `actions/dependency-review-action@v4` triggered on `pull_request`
instructions.original_snippets: Dependency Review: `actions/dependency-review-action@v4`
instructions.relevant_when: When adding dependency scanning or security review to a PR workflow
